core_mechanism: a geometric variational (extremization) principle in which a quantity is computed as the extremum of a cost functional over competing candidate boundary configurations — including a disconnected 'island' candidate — with the selected extremum proven equivalent to an alternative extremization scheme in a limiting regime
brief_reason: The abstract computes fine-grained entanglement entropy by extremizing surface/island prescriptions (defect extremal surface vs. island formula) and shows the two extremization schemes coincide in the large-brane-tension (weak-coupling) limit, which is a functional-extremum-over-candidate-configurations mechanism.
